macro_events.event_summary: Crude oil falls to a three-month low as markets price in a potential US-Iran peace deal, implying easing of sanctions and more Iranian barrels returning to global supply.
primary_drivers: Lower crude prices compress E&P revenue and margins
Risk premium unwind removes geopolitical bid from oil
Lower jet fuel and diesel input costs benefit transport/airlines
Potential sanctions relief increases global oil supply

beneficiaries.rationale: Jet fuel is ~20-25% of operating costs; UAL has high international long-haul exposure where fuel burn is largest, so a sustained drop in crude flows directly to margin expansion.
Same jet fuel cost lever as UAL; Delta also owns the Trainer refinery so crack spread dynamics matter, but net effect of lower crude is positive for unit cost guidance.
Most fuel-cost-sensitive of the big 3 due to weakest balance sheet and no fuel hedging program; every $1 drop in jet fuel is outsized EPS leverage.
Domestic carrier with historically active fuel hedging; lower crude lowers forward fuel curve and pressures cost-per-ASM lower into 2H.
Ethane/naphtha-based petrochemical producer where lower oil-linked naphtha feedstock improves global cost-curve position and polyethylene spreads.
Diesel and jet fuel are major opex; lower crude reduces Ground and Express fuel surcharges' cost base, supporting margin recovery thesis.

losers.rationale: Integrated major with large upstream exposure; lower oil prices directly hit upstream cash flow and the buyback/dividend coverage narrative, even if downstream offsets partially.
Similar upstream-heavy mix to XOM; Permian and international production revenue scales with crude, hurting near-term EPS estimates.
Pure-play US E&P with high leverage post-Anadarko/CrownRock; Permian-focused production makes cash flow very sensitive to WTI, and Buffett-stake narrative weakens at sub-$70.
Diamondback is a near-pure Permian E&P with no refining offset; revenue is roughly linear with WTI, making it one of the highest-beta names to this move.
Largest oilfield services name; sustained sub-$70 crude pressures E&P capex budgets, hitting international and NAM service pricing into 2H26.
US-leveraged pressure pumping and completions exposure means rig count and frac spread guidance get cut if E&Ps trim budgets on lower deck.
Has direct Egypt/North Sea operations and historically high sensitivity to Brent; also a smaller-cap E&P that trades as a high-beta proxy on oil price moves.
Cheniere's long-term contracts are oil-linked for some Asian buyers; sustained lower oil narrows LNG arbitrage versus oil-indexed contracts and pressures spot LNG netbacks.

headlines: Oil drops to fresh three-month low as markets weigh US-Iran peace deal
